meta_question: Where did SARS-CoV-2 come from? The same evidence, read by six analyses under different priors, spanned 23 orders of magnitude, so the answer is prior-driven.

evidence.statement: the 155 December-2019 cases with location data had a median distance of 4.28 km to the Huanan market (P<0.001) against 16.11 km fo
evidence.source: Worobey et al. (2022), Science 377(6609):951-959 (Huanan market epicenter)

evidence.statement: of 923 environmental samples from the market, 74 (8.0%) were positive for SARS-CoV-2, and 87.5% (56/64) of inside-market positives
evidence.source: Liu et al. (2023), Nature 618:640-646 (environmental surveillance of the market)

evidence.statement: all five positive environmental samples from one wildlife stall contained raccoon-dog, bamboo-rat, and civet DNA exceeding human D
evidence.source: Crits-Christoph et al. (2024), Cell 187(18):5318-5329 (market wildlife genetic tracing)

evidence.statement: both SARS-CoV-2 lineages A and B are linked to the market: lineage B in market cases and samples, lineage A in a glove sample and
evidence.source: Pekar et al. (2022), Science 377(6609):960-966 (two lineages; erratum 2023)

evidence.statement: the SARS-CoV-2 polybasic furin cleavage site was created by a precise 12-nucleotide insertion (PRRA) at the S1/S2 junction, and it
evidence.source: Segreto & Deigin (2020), BioEssays 42(12):e2000240 (furin cleavage site structure)

evidence.statement: the arginine codon CGG occurs at only 3-5% frequency in sarbecoviruses, so the CGG-CGG pair in the furin insertion is rare, and bo
evidence.source: Romeu (2023), BMC Genomic Data 24:57; Holmes et al. (2021), Cell 184(19):4848 (CGG codons)

evidence.statement: the 2018 DEFUSE proposal to DARPA, by researchers including WIV collaborators, described inserting human-specific furin cleavage s
evidence.source: The Intercept (2021), DEFUSE leaked proposal; US House Oversight letter (2024)

evidence.statement: no live raccoon dogs, civets, or porcupines were tested for SARS-CoV-2 at the market, the animals having been cleared before sampl
evidence.source: Liu et al. (2023), Nature 618:640-646 (environmental surveillance of the market)

evidence.statement: about one million wild mammals on Hubei wildlife farms supplying the market were released, sold, or killed in early 2020 without S
evidence.source: Worobey et al. (2022), Science 377(6609):951-959 (Huanan market epicenter)

interpretations.statement: that infected animals, not infected humans, deposited the market's viral RNA is argued from the wildlife-DNA samples, and is conte
interpretations.source: Crits-Christoph et al. (2024), Cell 187(18):5318-5329 (market wildlife genetic tracing)

interpretations.statement: that the two lineages record two separate zoonotic spillovers is contested: the Pekar Bayes factor fell from ~60 to ~4.3 after thr
interpretations.source: Weissman (2026), Econ Journal Watch 23(1); McCowan arXiv:2510.01484 (two-spillover reanalysis)

interpretations.statement: the furin site is functionally suboptimal and out-of-frame: natural-origin proponents read this as inconsistent with engineering,
interpretations.source: Holmes et al. (2021), Cell 184(19):4848-4856 (FCS suboptimality critical review)

interpretations.statement: that unlinked cases sat closer to the market than linked cases reveals proximity ascertainment bias, so the clustering may reflect
interpretations.source: Weissman (2024), JRSS-A 187(3):720 (proximity ascertainment bias); Debarre/Worobey reply

interpretations.statement: a reanalysis found SARS-CoV-2 abundance correlated most with implausible hosts (fish, cow), so the market-wide metagenomic content
interpretations.source: Bloom (2023), Virus Evolution 9(2):vead050 (metagenomic reanalysis); Debarre rebuttal

interpretations.statement: that a centroid of early cases must be the origin is unproved and the Monte Carlo null was flawed, though under a corrected implem
interpretations.source: Stoyan & Chiu (2024), JRSS-A; Debarre & Worobey arXiv:2403.05859 (centroid critique)

priors.statement: Miller's prior: a zoonotic pandemic starts in any given year with about 1% probability (one natural pandemic per century), a histo
priors.source: Shin Jie Yong (2024), Medium, 'My Friend Won the US$100,000 Debate' (Miller's priors)

priors.statement: Miller's prior: a lab-leak origin at about 1 in 588 (0.0005 that a lab made such a virus in 2019, times 0.05 that it leaked)
priors.source: Shin Jie Yong (2024), Medium, 'My Friend Won the US$100,000 Debate' (Miller's priors)

priors.statement: Rootclaim's prior: the market early cluster is only 2x evidence (near-neutral), since markets repeatedly form early COVID clusters
priors.source: Rootclaim Blog (2024), debate results and response to Scott Alexander (Rootclaim's priors)

priors.statement: Rootclaim's prior: a researcher working on a SARS-CoV-2-like virus under BSL-2 has about a 15% annual infection probability, and t
priors.source: Rootclaim Blog (2024), debate results and response to Scott Alexander (Rootclaim's priors)

meta.statement: six independent Bayesian analyses of the same evidence spanned 23.4 orders of magnitude, from Miller's pro-zoonosis extreme to Roo
meta.source: Scott Alexander (2024), Astral Codex Ten, 'Practically-A-Book-Review: Rootclaim Lab Leak Debate'

meta.statement: a confident origin verdict performs a settlement the evidence does not carry: with a 23-order spread across analysts, the conclusi
meta.source: Scott Alexander (2024), Astral Codex Ten, 'Practically-A-Book-Review: Rootclaim Lab Leak Debate'

meta.statement: both judges ruled for zoonosis under their own priors (Van Treuren ~1 in 300, Stansifer ~1 in 3000) while treating the Bayesian ca
meta.source: Judge Will Van Treuren, written decision (Feb 2024): ~1 in 300 lab leak

meta.statement: the conclusion is highly sensitive to the ordering and independence of the evidence: heroic Bayesian analysis multiplies correlate
meta.source: Scott Alexander (2024), Astral Codex Ten, 'Practically-A-Book-Review: Rootclaim Lab Leak Debate'

meta.statement: as of 2025 the origin remains genuinely unresolved: no decisive ancestor virus, records, or engineering signature has emerged, and
meta.source: WHO SAGO report (2025); ODNI declassified assessment; Gelman (2025) (unresolved)

zoonosis.statement: zoonotic spillover at the market is the more likely origin (Peter Miller's conclusion), resting on the shared evidence read under
zoonosis.source: Scott Alexander (2024), Astral Codex Ten, 'Practically-A-Book-Review: Rootclaim Lab Leak Debate'

lableak.statement: a research-related lab leak is the more likely origin (Rootclaim's conclusion), resting on the same shared evidence read under its
lableak.source: Rootclaim Blog (2024), debate results and response to Scott Alexander (Rootclaim's priors)

crux.note: the frontier is the crux the structure points at, a candidate set, not a decided crux; which candidate is decisive is a judgment the record invites.
